summary: Run role match-selection over the parsed roster: a `required` single-filler role with zero or many matching artifacts is a conformance error, wired into check

new.description: The harness-contract conformance pass over the parsed roster. For each `Role`, evaluate its `MatchSelector` against the workspace artifacts of the role's `artifact` kind â€” a minimal in-crate `*` glob over artifact names, or the `role` marker an artifact declares (a `role:` frontmatter field, read off `Features`) â€” and emit a precise error-severity `Diagnostic` when a `required` single-filler role is filled by zero, or by many, artifacts. Selection only: `conforms-to` the role contract and `verified_by` admissibility are follow-on. Module header cites `specs/10-contracts.md` 'Roles and matching'.

edit.description: Add `pub mod roster;` beside the existing module declarations (this entry owns the module's creation and its declaration line).

In `Check`, after loading the optional `AuthorLayer`, group the per-kind `Features` (skill, rule) and run `roster::check` over `layer.roles()`, extending the diagnostic set before the exit-code gate. Absent `temper.toml` (no layer / empty roster) adds nothing â€” the floor-only path stays byte-for-byte unchanged.

tests.asserts: End-to-end via the binary: a `temper.toml` declaring a `required` role matching zero artifacts fails check with a precise finding; exactly one match passes; two matches fail (single-filler); a non-`required` unfilled role is silent; no roster leaves the outcome unchanged.
Unit: `*`-glob and `role`-marker matching pick the right fillers; zero/one/many counts map to error/clean/error for a `required` role; a non-`required` role never fires.

acceptance: `temper check` gates the harness-contract roster: a `required` single-filler role with zero or many matching artifacts is an error-severity finding that exits non-zero; one match and absent/non-required roles are clean. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo fmt --all --check` green.
notes: Selection only. The `match` glob is a minimal in-crate `*` matcher â€” no glob crate joins the sanctioned set; the `role` marker matches a `role:` frontmatter field. Candidates come from loaded kinds (skill, rule); a required role over an unmodeled kind finds zero and fails â€” honest. Builds on the shipped parse foundation in compose.rs. `conforms-to` and `verified_by` admissibility stay frontier.
